name: Valle de la Gloria MTB Route
name_original: Valle de la Gloria – circular desde Angüés
description: This moderate 29.6-mile mountain biking route from Angüés explores the Valle de la Gloria, passing historic monasteries and charming village
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Angüés are situated in the Hoya de Huesca region of Aragon, Spain, offering diverse and dramatic natural landscapes. The area serves as a gateway to the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, characterized by rugged mountains, intricate gorges, and canyons. The karstic topography features limestone and sandstone formations, with rivers like the Alcanadre carving out deep ravines and creating natural pools, alongside extensive pine and holm oak forests. Best mountain bike trails around Angüés The most popular mountain bike trail is Valle de la Gloria MTB Route, a 29.6 miles (47.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic riding through a picturesque valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ermita de Treviño y Azlor – circular desde Abiego, a moderate 15.2 miles (24.5 km) path. This circular route explores the rural landscapes around the hermitage and Azlor. Local mountain bikers also love the Hoya de Huesca – circular por Blecua y Antillón, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail leading through the varied terrain of the Hoya de Huesca, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Angüés is defined by rugged mountains, deep gorges and canyons, and extensive pine and holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Angüés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Angüés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Angüés?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Angüés, offering a diverse range of experiences. The area is known for its rugged mountains, deep gorges, and extensive pine and holm oak forests, providing varied terrain for riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails near Angüés?
A: The mountain bike trails around Angüés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic routes through picturesque valleys, and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty of the Hoya de Huesca region and the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners near Angüés?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy mountain bike trails around Angüés. These routes offer a good starting point for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride through the scenic landscapes of the Hoya de Huesca.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 2 difficult mountain bike trails around Angüés. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and technical sections, taking you through the rugged mountains and deep ravines characteristic of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over canyon – View of Huevo de Morrano loop from Bierge&lt;/a&gt;, which includes nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Angüés?
A: Yes, several circular mountain bike routes are available. These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics easier. Popular options include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976702984/ermita-de-trevino-y-azlor-circular-desde-abiego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Treviño y Azlor – circular desde Abiego&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964794131/hoya-de-huesca-circular-por-blecua-y-antillon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya de Huesca – circular por Blecua y Antillón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails near Angüés?
A: The region around Angüés is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge&lt;/a&gt;, a picturesque waterfall and popular bathing spot, or the tranquil, turquoise waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamara Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The area is part of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, known for its intricate network of gorges and canyons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de Tamara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the area combines natural beauty with historical significance. You might pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5254839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montearagón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th- and 12th-century Romanesque fortress. Another notable religious site is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2983128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Beautiful Hermitage of Treviño&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19886015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Azlor – The Beautiful Hermitage of Treviño loop from Abiego&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Angüés?
A: The Huesca Pre-Pyrenees, where Angüés is located, offers excellent mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While specific winter conditions vary, the region's diverse terrain means some trails may be accessible even in cooler months, though higher elevations might be affected by snow.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails near Angüés?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed in the guide, the presence of easy trails suggests options for families. The varied terrain of the Hoya de Huesca and the Sierra de Guara includes paths suitable for different skill levels, so looking for shorter, less technical routes would be a good approach for family outings.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain biking areas by public transport from Angüés?
A: Angüés is situated in a rural area, and public transport options directly to trailheads within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park or the Hoya de Huesca may be limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or consider personal vehicle transport for convenient access to the mountain biking routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails around Angüés?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, many routes in the Hoya de Huesca and Sierra de Guara regions typically have designated parking areas near their starting points, especially in or near villages. It's recommended to check the individual route descriptions on komoot for specific parking information before you set out.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass through a valley?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977143916/valle-de-la-gloria-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de la Gloria MTB Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that offers scenic riding through a picturesque valley. This route provides a moderate challenge over nearly 48 km, allowing you to immerse yourself in the beautiful natural surroundings.
tips: This church is declared as a national monument. It is isolated, in the middle of the field and with the Sierra de Guara as the only reference, in the background. It was ordered to be built as a family pantheon by Ximeno de Foces in the 13th century. This family was also the builder of the Valencia Cathedral. To visit its interior, contact the Ibieca City Council.
Near the parish church, the remains of the wall and the old castle of the town stand out, in a clearly strategic position.

Despite the numerous reforms that were carried out later, the temple still retains its medieval structure. The refectory, the chapter house, the cloister and the portal of its church will especially catch your attention.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Valle de la Gloria circular route from Angüés?
faqs.answer: While the route can be started from several villages, Angüés is a feasible starting point. You can look for parking within the village itself. The nearby village of Velillas, part of the Angüés municipality, is also noted as a convenient starting point due to its proximity to the main road.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Valle de la Gloria circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of tracks, with no singletrack sections. It features relatively flat terrain overall but includes constant ups and downs, providing a varied ride. Be aware that shade is limited, especially during summer.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Valle de la Gloria circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It has a medium physical difficulty due to its length (approximately 47 km), requiring a minimum level of fitness. However, the technical difficulty is considered low, making it accessible to a broader range of riders.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the Valle de la Gloria route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the main towns of the valley and past several hermitages, including San Miguel de Foces. You'll also pass by the Church of San Martín de Foces, the Labata Village and Church, and the Monasterio de Casbas. The valley itself is named after the Cistercian monastery of Nuestra Señora de la Gloria.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Valle de la Gloria circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail is suitable for riding throughout the year. However, due to limited shade, it's advisable to avoid the hottest parts of the day, especially during summer. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for this route.

faqs.question: Is the Valle de la Gloria – circular desde Angüés trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this route. As it passes through natural areas, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when near livestock or wildlife, and always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there water stops available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Angüés is noted as offering water stops, and other villages along the route may also provide opportunities to refill your water bottles. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water, especially given the limited shade.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route coincides with sections of several official trails. Notably, it covers about 21% of the Camino Natural de la Hoya de Huesca and also includes parts of the Camino de Ibieca a Angüés and the Camino de Sieso a Ibieca, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and limited shade, essential items include plenty of water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat), and snacks. A repair kit for your bike is also recommended. While the route is marked as 'Ruta Local 1 Valle de la Gloria,' some signage may be damaged, so a GPS device for navigation is highly advisable.

faqs.question: Can the Valle de la Gloria route be shortened?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route can be shortened by opting out of some of its loops. This flexibility allows riders to adjust the distance and duration to better suit their fitness levels or available time.

waypoints.name: Church of San Martín de Foces
